verses.commentary: I urge, then, first of all establishes prayer as priority. That petitions, prayers, intercession and thanksgiving be made for all people lists four prayer types. Universal scope: prayer encompasses everyone, not merely believers.

For kings and all those in authority specifies prayer for rulers. That we may live peaceful and quiet lives states purpose: social stability enabling godliness. In all godliness and holiness describes the life sought: reverence and purity.
This is good, and pleases God our Savior affirms prayer for all as divinely approved. God's pleasure validates the practice, grounding ethics in theology.

Who wants all people to be saved reveals God's universal salvific desire. And to come to a knowledge of the truth defines salvation: knowing truth, not merely assenting to propositions. God's will is universal gospel availability, though not universal salvation.

For there is one God establishes monotheism. And one mediator between God and mankind specifies Christ's exclusive role. The man Christ Jesus emphasizes the incarnation: humanity required for mediation. One God, one mediator equals one way.

Who gave himself as a ransom for all people states atonement's nature and scope. This has now been witnessed to at the proper time marks historical accomplishment at divinely appointed moment. Ransom implies purchase from bondage.

And for this purpose I was appointed a herald and an apostle lists Paul's dual role: announcer and authoritative messenger. I am telling the truth, I am not lying protests authenticity. And a true and faithful teacher of the Gentiles specifies his mission field.

Therefore I want the men everywhere to pray draws application. Lifting up holy hands describes prayer posture: open, reaching, clean. Without anger or disputing qualifies attitude: prayer requires relational peace and internal purity.

I also want the women to dress modestly shifts focus to women. With decency and propriety adds qualifiers. Adorning themselves, not with elaborate hairstyles or gold or pearls or expensive clothes prohibits ostentatious display, addressing cultural context of status competition through appearance.

But with good deeds, appropriate for women who profess to worship God contrasts external with internal adornment. True beauty is moral, not cosmetic. Profession of worship demands consistency in life.

A woman should learn in quietness and full submission addresses women's learning, revolutionary in that culture. Quietness means receptive spirit, not absolute silence. Full submission qualifies the learning posture.

I do not permit a woman to teach or to assume authority over a man restricts specific roles in church gathering. She must be quiet reiterates verse 11. The ground and scope of this restriction remain debated: universal principle or cultural application.
For Adam was formed first, then Eve appeals to creation order as rationale. Priority in creation suggests order in function, though the argument's force and scope are contested.

And Adam was not the one deceived; it was the woman who was deceived and became a sinner cites the fall narrative. The argument seems to be about susceptibility to deception, though Eve's sin came through deception while Adam's was direct disobedience.

But women will be saved through childbearing is notoriously difficult. Not saved by childbearing as works-righteousness. Perhaps: preserved through the dangers of childbirth, or saved despite the curse, or fulfilled through motherhood. If they continue in faith, love and holiness with propriety conditions the promise on persevering faith. The verse resists easy interpretation.
